evals.prompt: Write a TypeScript function that fetches a user from a REST API by ID and returns their profile. The API endpoint is /api/users/:id and returns JSON with fields id, name, email, and role (either 'admin' or 'user'). Handle the case where the user doesn't exist.
evals.expected_output: A well-typed async function with explicit Promise<User | null> return type, a typed User interface with a 'role' union literal, no 'any', a null return for 404, and inline documentation of any type assertions used.
expectations: Defines a typed User interface or type with id, name, email, and role fields
role is typed as a union literal 'admin' | 'user', not a raw string
The function has an explicit Promise<User | null> return type annotation
No use of 'any' in the function signature or body
The 404 / not-found case returns null rather than throwing
Any 'as' type assertion is accompanied by a justifying comment

evals.expected_output: Refactored code replacing 'any' with generics, removing unexplained non-null assertion, adding explicit return types on exported functions, and adding a runtime type guard for the JSON.parse result.
expectations: processItems is rewritten with generic type parameters instead of 'any[]' and 'any' callback
processItems has an explicit return type annotation
findById is rewritten with a generic or typed parameter instead of 'any[]'
findById has an explicit return type annotation
The non-null assertion '!' on localStorage.getItem is removed or documented
The 'as' cast on JSON.parse is replaced with or supplemented by a runtime type guard

evals.prompt: Design TypeScript types for a form that can be in one of three states: loading (spinner shown), success (form data displayed), or error (error message shown). Then write a render function that takes this state and returns a string description of what to display. Make it impossible to represent states like 'loading AND error' simultaneously.
evals.expected_output: A discriminated union type with a literal 'status' or 'kind' field, a render function with exhaustive switch/case, and explicit typed return values — no optional fields on a flat object.
expectations: Uses a discriminated union type (not a flat object with optional fields)
Each variant has a literal discriminant field (e.g. status: 'loading')
The render function has an explicit return type annotation
The switch/case covers all union members exhaustively
No use of 'any' in types or the render function
